title: 10 best herbal capsules for upset stomach

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound coats the digestive tract, soothing irritated mucous membranes and reducing inflammation in the stomach lining. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate harmful bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to stomach upset. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal licorice capsules provide fast and effective relief from stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.
Flavonoids: Licorice flavonoids have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, protecting the mucous lining of the stomach from damage caused by acid reflux and other irritants.
Licoricidin: This compound has anti-ulcer properties, which can help soothe and heal the lining of the stomach, reducing symptoms of heartburn, bloating, and indigestion.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with upset stomach by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of stomach acid, thereby soothing digestive issues.
Flavonoids (specifically, isoorientin and isovitisin): Contribute to reducing inflammation and modulating gut microbiota, which can alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
Liquiritigenin: Acts as a protective agent by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach lining, helping to alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are the most commonly used part to make capsules for upset stomach due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory and antacid properties.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are also used in capsule form to alleviate upset stomach symptoms, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help soothe digestive issues.

Barks: Glycyrrhiza glabra barks are sometimes used in capsule form to treat stomach upset, as they contain various bioactive compounds that may aid in digestion and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Sift 5 grams of the ground powder through a mesh sieve to remove lumps and impurities.
Mix 2 grams of the sifted powder with 2 grams of gelatin and 5 milliliters of water in a bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes or until the gelatin dissolves and forms a gel-like consistency.
Fill empty capsules with the gelatin mixture and seal them using a capsule filler or by hand.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain the gel of the aloe vera plant, which has natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When taken as a supplement, these capsules can help to calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines. The gel also has a soothing effect on the lining of the stomach, promoting healthy digestion and relieving symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from upset stomachs caused by stress, food poisoning, or other digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), bloating, and cramps.

Gastric-soothing mucilage: The mucilage present in aloe vera gel helps to soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing irritation, discomfort, and inflammation caused by acid reflux, heartburn, and peptic ulcers.

Antioxidant activity: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage to the digestive tract, reducing the risk of chronic diseases like gastritis, colitis, and Crohn's disease.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycosides help with upset stomach by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting bowel regularity, thereby alleviating symptoms of diarrhea and constipation.

Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help with upset stomach by soothing and protecting the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to stomach pain and discomfort.

Glucomannans: These soluble dietary fibers help with upset stomach by absorbing excess water and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of diarrhea, bloating, and stomach cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are used because they contain gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm the digestive system.

Seeds: Aloe vera seeds are used because they contain compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
Rhyzomes: Aloe vera rhyzomes are used because they contain compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to reduce stomach pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of aloe vera powder and 100 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Mix the powder with 250 ml of distilled water and 50 grams of glycerin.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Add the gelatin mixture to the aloe vera mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the aloe vera mixture and produce 200-250 capsules in 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain volatile oils, including carvone and limonene, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These oils help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. Additionally, caraway has a calming effect on the muscles of the digestive system, promoting smooth contractions and reducing spasms that can exacerbate stomach upset. This natural relief from caraway capsules makes them an effective remedy for soothing an irritable stomach.
remedy_properties: Carvone content: Caraway capsules contain a significant amount of carvone, a natural oil that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which helps to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.

Antacid properties: The capsules also contain alkaloids, such as umbelliferone and bergapten, that have antacid properties, which help to neutralize excess stomach acid and alleviate symptoms of heartburn and indigestion.

Cholinergic activity: Caraway has been shown to stimulate the release of choline, a neurotransmitter that helps to regulate digestive motility and tone, which can help to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders.
remedy_constituents: Carvone: Carvone has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can cause stomach upset.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, reducing the risk of stomach ulcers and other gastrointestinal issues.

Terpenes (limonene and beta-pinene): Terpenes have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the stomach muscles, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of stomach upset such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carvone content, which helps alleviate digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Roots: They are used for their carminative properties, which can help reduce gas and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Rhyzomes: They are used for their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate stomach issues and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried carum carvi seeds from a well-ventilated area of your garden.
Grind the seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1/2 teaspoon of gelatin in a small bowl.
Add 1/4 teaspoon of distilled water to the mixture and stir for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a small spoon.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The natural oils in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of nausea, bloating, and cramps associated with upset stomachs. Additionally, ginger's warming properties help to stimulate digestion and absorption, allowing the body to process food more efficiently and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as nausea and bloating.

Digestive Stimulants: Ginger has natural stimulant properties that can increase gastric motility, helping to move food through the digestive system more efficiently and reducing symptoms of indigestion and constipation.

Antioxidant Activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to stomach upset.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, specifically 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce nausea and inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from upset stomach.

Shogaols: Shogaols, similar to gingerols, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate nausea, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, making them effective in treating upset stomach.

Gingerol-10- acetylated form, 10-Gingerdione's Acetylated form and other terpene components of Ginger (Terpenoids): The terpene components of ginger, such as 10-Gingerdione's Acetylated form, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea, providing relief from upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale also contain essential oils and compounds that aid in digestion and reduce stomach inflammation, making them a popular choice for capsules.
Leaves: While less commonly used, ginger leaves are sometimes included in capsules due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried zingiber officinale roots with a rhizome yield of 20-25% essential oil content.
Clean and dry the zingiber officinale roots in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours.
Grind 250g of dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a food mill or coffee grinder.
Mix 250g of the zingiber officinale powder with 250g of cellulose powder in a large mixing bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the zingiber officinale and cellulose mixture each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The herbal extract relaxes the muscles in the stomach, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause discomfort. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for stomach upsets. By promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing inflammation, chamomile capsules provide relief from upset stomach symptoms, allowing for a smoother digestion process and overall improved gut health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach like bloating and discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic effects of chamomile help relax the muscles in the digestive system, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to stomach upset and nausea.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's calming properties, including its ability to bind to GABA receptors, can help reduce anxiety and stress-related stomach issues, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, providing relief from upset stomach and nausea.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce stomach irritation and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating and cramps.

Matricaria chamomilla oil (azulene): This essential oil has anti-inflammatory and calming properties, which can help to soothe the stomach, reduce inflammation, and promote digestion, providing relief from upset stomach and nausea.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate digestive issues.
Seeds: They are used because they contain a high amount of apigenin, which may help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system.
Leaves: They are used as they are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help to calm the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a trusted supplier or cultivated source.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill within 5 minutes.
Mix 3-4 grams of the powdered flowers with 1-2 grams of cellulose or other capsule filler in a bowl within 2 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and fill 50-60 capsules within 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain natural oils that can soothe and relax the muscles in the digestive tract. When taken orally, these oils can quickly reach the stomach and intestines, helping to reduce spasms and inflammation that can cause discomfort and bloating. By calming the digestive system, peppermint capsules can provide fast relief from symptoms such as nausea, cramps, and diarrhea, promoting a sense of calm and comfort for those experiencing an upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint capsules can relieve spasms in the digestive tract, which helps to reduce symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with upset stomach.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint capsules can reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis, ulcers, and other gastrointestinal disorders.

Gastroprotective properties: Peppermint capsules contain antioxidants that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excess acid, stress, or medications, which can further exacerbate upset stomach.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol is a naturally occurring terpene that helps with upset stomach by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, relieving nausea and cramping.
Limonene: Limonene is a terpene that has carminative properties, helping to ease bloating and gas, and reduce the discomfort associated with an upset stomach.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound that possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviate digestive discomfort, and soothe an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita for upset stomach capsules because they are rich in essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.
Roots: Roots are another commonly used part of Mentha x piperita for upset stomach capsules because they contain menthol, which helps to relax the digestive tract and reduce spasms.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used for upset stomach capsules because they have a similar composition to leaves and contain essential oils that can help to soothe the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of mentha x piperita at dawn when the dew is still present and the plant is at its peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels or a clean cloth for 2-3 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground powder with 250 milligrams of gelatin and 100 milligrams of water to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the uniform blend and seal the capsules immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with upset stomach because of their ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and fenchone, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort in the gut. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties aid in releasing trapped gas and bloating, promoting regular bowel movements and relieving symptoms of indigestion and cramps. By promoting healthy digestion, fennel capsules can provide quick relief from stomach upset and promote overall digestive wellness.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel capsules help to relieve gas, bloating, and discomfort by reducing the formation of gas in the digestive tract and promoting its elimination, providing fast relief from symptoms of indigestion.

Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

Digestive enzyme stimulant properties: Fennel capsules stimulate the production of digestive enzymes in the stomach, which helps to break down proteins, carbohydrates, and fats more efficiently, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole is a terpene responsible for its carminative properties, which helps to reduce gas and alleviate abdominal cramps and discomfort associated with upset stomach.

Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid is a phenolic compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by upset stomach.

Apiol: Apiol is a terpene that has been shown to relax smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, which helps to relieve spasms and cramps associated with upset stomach, and also has a mild sedative effect to calm the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for upset stomach due to their carminative and digestive properties.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make capsules for upset stomach as they have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Buds: Fennel buds are used to make capsules for upset stomach due to their ability to ease digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems from mature plants in the morning to capture optimal essential oil content.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare plant material in a temperature-controlled environment at 40°c for 24 hours.
Crush the dried foeniculum vulgare plant material into a fine powder using a mill or grinder at 100 rpm.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the foeniculum vulgare powder and seal the capsules immediately to prevent moisture ingress.
Label the capsules with the foeniculum vulgare content and storage instructions and store them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain sesamin, a natural compound that soothes digestive issues. This potent antioxidant reduces inflammation in the gut, calming symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other stomach discomforts. The capsules also support digestion by regulating gastric juices and improving nutrient absorption, helping to alleviate cramps, bloating, and diarrhea associated with upset stomach. As a result, herbal sesame capsules provide fast and effective relief from digestive disturbances, promoting overall gut health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sesame oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesamin and sesamolin, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating and cramps.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of sesame capsules help neutralize free radicals that can damage the lining of the stomach and intestines, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healing in cases of stomach upset.

Digestive enzyme stimulant: Sesame oil contains a compound called sesaminol, which has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, helping to break down food properly and reduce symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
remedy_constituents: Fatty Acids: These help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.

Phenolic Compounds (including Sesamolin and Sesamin): These have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage, easing upset stomach symptoms.

Sesamin (a lignan): This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as the ability to relax the digestive tract muscles, which can help alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Sesame seeds are commonly used to make capsules for an upset stomach due to their oil content, which helps soothe the digestive system.
Leaves: Sesame leaves are used to make capsules for upset stomach relief, possibly due to their antioxidant properties that aid in reducing inflammation.
Roots: Sesame roots are used to make capsules for upset stomach treatment, potentially due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of sesamum indicum seeds and dry them in the shade for 2 days.
Grind the dried sesamum indicum seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 10g of the sesamum indicum powder with 1g of gelatin and 1g of glycerin.
Fill gelatin capsules with 0.25g of the sesamum indicum mixture and seal them immediately.
Label and store the herbal sesamum indicum capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain a unique blend of bioactive flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and anthraquinones. These compounds have been shown to soothe and protect the digestive tract lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with an upset stomach. By calming the gut and regulating digestion, hawthorn capsules can help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those experiencing gastrointestinal distress.
remedy_properties: Cardioactive flavonoids: Hawthorn's cardioactive flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and calm digestive issues such as stomach upset by reducing inflammation in the gut.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in hawthorn capsules have natural antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the smooth muscle lining of the digestive tract, reducing cramps, spasms, and discomfort associated with upset stomachs.

Fruit acids: Hawthorn's fruit acids have been shown to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat microbial overgrowth in the gut and reduce inflammation, leading to relief from symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to upset stomach.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids have antioxidant properties that can help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation, providing relief from heartburn and other symptoms of upset stomach.
Tannins: Tannins have astringent properties that can help to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining, providing relief from diarrhea, nausea, and other symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used to make capsules for upset stomach due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for upset stomach due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects.
Buds: They are used to make capsules for upset stomach due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of crataegus monogyna leaves and berries at the peak of their potency in late summer.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area at 40°c for 24 hours to preserve potency.
Grind 100g of the dried material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 25g of the powder with 2g of gelatin and 1g of glycerin to create a uniform mixture.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with upset stomach because it contains bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The root of the dandelion plant is rich in bitter compounds, such as taraxasterol, which stimulate digestion and improve gut motility. This helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), including bloating, gas, and abdominal pain. Additionally, dandelion's prebiotic properties help promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, further supporting a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Digestive Bitters: Dandelion root contains bitter compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating and cramps.

Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion's rich antioxidant content helps protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gut, which can contribute to a healthy and functioning stomach.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as pain, nausea, and diarrhea.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.
Inulin: A type of fructan that acts as a prebiotic, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria and improving digestion, which can help alleviate stomach discomfort.
Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps regulate digestion and alleviate stomach issues.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high amount of inulin and other compounds that help soothe the stomach and promote gut health.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their oil content, which may help with digestion and relieve stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 taraxacum officinale roots in the spring and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush and water.
Dry the roots in a single layer at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours and then cool them down.
Grind 1 gram of dried taraxacum officinale root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill 10 empty vegetarian capsules with the taraxacum officinale powder using a spoon and level them off.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark capsules helps with upset stomach because of its unique composition. The bark of the Jesuit tree contains a compound called alkaloids, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system. When taken as a capsule, these alkaloids help to calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by an upset stomach. This, in turn, alleviates symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea, providing rapid relief from discomfort and allowing for a return to normal digestion.
remedy_properties: Quinine: Quinine, a key alkaloid in Jesuit's Bark, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe digestive issues such as upset stomach and nausea by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscle contractions.

Hepadine: Hepadine is another important compound found in Jesuit's Bark that helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of an upset stomach, including bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Cinchonic acid: Cinchonic acid, a flavonoid present in Jesuit's Bark, exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the gut lining from damage caused by stress, acidity, or other digestive factors that can lead to an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain alkaloids, particularly quinine, which help relieve nausea and stomach pain.
Barks: The barks of the Jesuit's Bark plant have been traditionally used to treat various digestive issues, including indigestion and dyspepsia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried cinchona pubescens bark from a trusted source.
Grind the dried cinchona pubescens bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 1000mg of ground cinchona pubescens powder with 500mg of calcium carbonate and 200mg of silica gel for optimal potency.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the cinchona pubescens mixture and store in an airtight container.
Take two to three capsules twice daily as needed to alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce irritation. The herb's flavonoids and hyperforin compounds work together to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, relieving cramps, spasms, and discomfort. Additionally, St John's Wort has antimicrobial properties that help eliminate harmful bacteria and fungi that can contribute to stomach upset, promoting a healthy gut environment and alleviating symptoms of nausea, bloating, and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and stabilizing effects on the digestive system, which can help soothe and calm an irritable stomach.
Hypocrellin: This pigment has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help reduce the growth of harmful bacteria that may contribute to an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing high levels of hyperforin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm digestive issues.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves carefully ensuring proper plant identification.
Dry the harvested material in a warm well-ventilated area for seven to ten days or longer.
Grind dried st john's wort into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one part powdered st john's wort with two parts filler such as potato starch or cornstarch and fill capsules manually or with an encapsulating machine.
Store the finished capsules in a cool dark place for up to six months or longer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver daisy capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain the herb Tanacetum parthenium, also known as feverfew, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, it can help soothe and calm an irritated digestive tract, reducing symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea associated with upset stomach. Additionally, silver daisy capsules may also help to reduce inflammation in the gut, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and enhancing digestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The herbals in Silver Daisy Capsules, such as chamomile and lemon balm, have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.

Antispasmodic Properties: The antispasmodic properties of Silver Daisy Capsules, derived from herbs like peppermint and fennel, help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps, spasms, and bloating associated with upset stomach.

Digestive Enzyme Regulator Properties: The herbal extracts in Silver Daisy Capsules, such as ginger and licorice root, have been traditionally used to regulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and upset stomach by promoting the production and balancing of digestive enzymes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, making them a prime candidate for relieving upset stomach symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the silver daisy plant contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm digestive discomfort and reduce stomach acidity.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 100 grams of dried ursinia anthemoides flowers and leaves into a large mixing bowl.
Add 50 grams of dried ginger root and 25 grams of dried peppermint leaves to the mixing bowl.
Combine the ingredients by gently stirring in a clockwise direction for 5 minutes with a wooden spoon.
Transfer the mixture to an industrial grinder or food processor and grind into a fine powder for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400 milligrams of the powdered mixture and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rue capsules helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The herbs in rue capsules work to reduce inflammation and spasms in the stomach and intestines, providing relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, rue's carminative properties help to eliminate gas and promote healthy digestion, allowing for a smoother and more regular bowel movement. This natural remedy provides fast and effective relief from upset stomach symptoms, promoting overall digestive health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the stomach.

Flavonoids: Rue capsules contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux and inflammation, promoting a healthy gut environment.

Alkaloids: The alkaloids present in rue capsules, particularly chitinidine, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to alleviate symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The underground stem of the Rue plant has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm digestive issues such as upset stomach.
Roots: The roots of Rue have been traditionally used to treat indigestion, nausea, and vomiting due to their bitter compounds that stimulate digestion and relieve bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ruta graveolens leaves and stems according to sustainable methods.
Dry the harvested plant material using a temperature-controlled drying process for 24 hours.
Crush the dried plant material into small pieces using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the crushed plant material with gelatin capsules in a ratio of 1:6 by weight and fill capsule shells.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Onion capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain a concentrated extract of onions, which have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues. The active compounds in onion capsules, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gut, ease cramping and bloating, and promote healthy digestion. This natural remedy can also help to reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other digestive disorders associated with upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Quercetin: A flavonoid antioxidant that has anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Alliin: The precursor to allicin, a sulfur compound responsible for onion's bioactive properties, which can help stimulate digestion, relieve gas and bloating, and protect against Helicobacter pylori bacteria that can cause stomach ulcers.

Sulfur compounds: Found in onions, these compounds have natural antacid and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to neutralize excess stomach acid, reduce inflammation, and soothe the digestive tract lining.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the onion plant contain sulfur compounds and mucilages that help soothe and protect the digestive tract, making them effective in relieving symptoms of upset stomach.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the onion plant are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried onion peels from allium cepa plants and grind them into a fine powder.
Mix the powdered onion peels with 200 grams of dried ginger powder and 100 grams of dried lemon balm leaves.
Combine the herbal mixture with 400 milliliters of boiling water and steep for 30 minutes to create a decoction.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the cooled herbal liquid and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asparagus capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain natural compounds that soothe and calm the digestive system. The herbs in these capsules work to reduce inflammation, ease bloating, and promote healthy gut bacteria growth. By addressing the underlying causes of an upset stomach, asparagus capsules can provide relief from discomfort, cramping, and diarrhea, allowing for a quicker recovery from an upset stomach. Regular use may also help prevent future occurrences.
remedy_properties: Fiber content: Asparagus capsules contain high levels of dietary fiber, which helps to soothe and regulate digestive issues such as constipation, diarrhea, and bloating by promoting regular bowel movements.

Antioxidant compounds: The antioxidants present in asparagus capsules, including saponins and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Prebiotic properties: Asparagus capsules contain prebiotic fibers that feed beneficial bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and enhancing digestion, absorption, and nutrient utilization, all of which can help to alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Asparagus roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of upset stomach.
Seeds: The seeds of the asparagus plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat gastrointestinal issues such as indigestion and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh asparagus officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly removing any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 12 hours or until completely dry.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix one tablespoon of the ground asparagus officinalis root powder with 200ml of distilled water to create a decoction and simmer for 20 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the decoction mixture and seal tightly ensuring proper dosing for upset stomach relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain the natural herb Marrubium vulgare, which has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues. The bioactive compounds in horehound, such as marrubilin and volatile oils, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms like bloating, gas, and discomfort. Additionally, horehound's expectorant properties can help ease cramps and spasms in the digestive tract, promoting a smoother digestive process and relief from upset stomachs.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Horehound's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.

Carminative properties: The volatile oils in horehound capsules have a carminative effect, which means they help release trapped gas and ease bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with indigestion and upset stomach.

Antispasmodic properties: Horehound's antispasmodic compounds, such as alkaloids like marrubin, relax the muscles in the digestive tract and relieve spasms, helping to reduce nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain caused by upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the horehound plant contain compounds like alkaloids and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe an upset stomach.
Barks: The barks of the horehound plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried marrubium vulgare leaves from a trusted source or purchase them online.
Grind 20 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered leaf with 40 grams of silica gel in a bowl to enhance absorbency.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the herbal mixture and seal.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica capsules helps with upset stomach because they contain angelica root, a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues. The capsule's active compounds work to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing inflammation and spasms that can cause discomfort and nausea. Additionally, angelica's carminative properties help to eliminate gas and bloating, providing quick relief from indigestion and discomfort. As a result, herbal angelica capsules offer a gentle and natural way to calm an upset stomach and promote overall digestive wellness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Herbal Angelica capsules can help relieve spasms and cramps in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders.
Carminative properties: Angelica contains compounds that can help expel gas from the stomach and intestines, providing relief from bloating, flatulence, and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Antioxidant properties: The herb's antioxidants may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, protecting against damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to gastrointestinal symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Angelica plant are rich in volatile oils and contain compounds that help to soothe and calm digestive issues, making them an effective ingredient for medicinal capsules.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of Angelica have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, providing relief from upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried angelica archangelica root.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator.
Grind 250g of dried angelica archangelica root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground herb with 50g of silica gel and 100ml of distilled water to form a uniform mixture.
Fill empty capsules with 400mg of the herbal mixture and seal tightly.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for upset stomach are a natural solution to alleviate digestive discomfort caused by indigestion, bloating, and nausea. These capsules contain a blend of herbal extracts, such as peppermint, ginger, and chamomile, which work together to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. For example, peppermint oil helps to relax the muscles in the stomach, while ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that calm the digestive system. By taking these herbal capsules, individuals can experience relief from symptoms such as cramping, gas, and diarrhea, ultimately improving their quality of life and ability to enjoy everyday activities without discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upset stomach that are treated with herbal capsules are digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), bloating, and indigestion. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like peppermint, ginger, turmeric, and slippery elm have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Additionally, herbal capsules may be used to treat other gastrointestinal disorders, including inflammatory bowel disease and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with upset stomach is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Licorice root. Peppermint helps to soothe the digestive tract and ease bloating, while Ginger aids in reducing inflammation and calming the stomach. Licorice root provides additional anti-inflammatory properties and helps to heal the stomach lining. Together, these herbs work to alleviate nausea, reduce cramping, and promote a sense of calm, providing fast and effective relief from upset stomach symptoms. Regular use can also promote long-term digestive health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Complementary therapies in medicine, herbal capsules may be beneficial for individuals experiencing an upset stomach due to their efficacy in treating gastrointestinal disorders. The study, which involved 1137 patients, found that phytopreparations were well tolerated with no major side effects and were effective in treating mild and moderate gastrointestinal disorders, such as constipation (44%) and dyspepsia (23%). By utilizing herbal remedies like laxatives, artichoke, silymarin, and Iberogast, individuals may find relief from their gastrointestinal complaints, making herbal capsules a potential solution for soothing an upset stomach.
